req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a daily transfer limit of $25,000 for internal (same-owner) account transfers. Key partitions are: (1) valid transfers below the limit, (2) transfers exactly at the limit, (3) transfers exceeding the limit, and (4) transfers with insufficient funds. Boundary values are $24,999.99 (below limit), $25,000.00 (at limit), and $25,000.01 (above limit). State transitions cover the account balance moving from sufficient to insufficient. A decision table captures the interaction between daily-limit and balance conditions. All tests are scoped to same-owner accounts per the precondition source.owner == target.owner.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
No prior transfers have been made today (daily_transferred == 0)
expected: Response body contains transfer_id and status='completed'
trace: R-FIN-003: transfer up to $25,000 per day shall succeed
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(auth_headers, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_balance_before = <get_balance>(src, auth_headers)
    tgt_balance_before = <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "1000.00"}
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "completed"
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    src_balance_after = <get_balance>(src, auth_headers)
    tgt_balance_after = <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers)
    assert src_balance_after == src_balance_before - 1000.00
    assert tgt_balance_after == tgt_balance_before + 1000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
No prior transfers have been made today (daily_transferred == 0)
expected: Response body contains error code DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
trace: R-FIN-003: transfers above $25,000 shall be declined
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_returns_422(auth_headers, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_balance_before = <get_balance>(src, auth_headers)
    tgt_balance_before = <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.01"}
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"] == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert <get_balance>(src, auth_headers) == src_balance_before
    assert <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers) == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_transfer_exactly_at_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
No prior transfers have been made today (daily_transferred == 0)
expected: Response body contains transfer_id and status='completed'
trace: R-FIN-003: transfer up to $25,000 per day (inclusive boundary)
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_exactly_at_daily_limit_succeeds(auth_headers, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_balance_before = <get_balance>(src, auth_headers)
    tgt_balance_before = <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.00"}
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "completed"
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert <get_balance>(src, auth_headers) == src_balance_before - 25000.00
    assert <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers) == tgt_balance_before + 25000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_one_cent_below_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
No prior transfers have been made today (daily_transferred == 0)
expected: Response body contains transfer_id and status='completed'
trace: R-FIN-003: transfer up to $25,000 per day — one cent below upper bound must succeed
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_one_cent_below_daily_limit_succeeds(auth_headers, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "24999.99"}
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "completed"
    assert "transfer_id" in body

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
No prior transfers have been made today (daily_transferred == 0)
expected: Response body contains error code INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
trace: R-FIN-003: funds moved atomically — implies rollback on failure
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(auth_headers, own_accounts_with_low_balance, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_with_low_balance
    src_balance_before = <get_balance>(src, auth_headers)
    tgt_balance_before = <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "500.00"}
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"] == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    assert <get_balance>(src, auth_headers) == src_balance_before
    assert <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers) == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_cumulative_transfers_exceeding_daily_limit_declined
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
A prior transfer of 20000.00 has already been completed today (daily_transferred == 20000.00)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=10000.00 (would bring daily total to 30000.00, exceeding limit)
expected: Response body contains error code DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
trace: R-FIN-003: $25,000 per day — daily limit is cumulative across multiple transfers
tests.executable:
def test_cumulative_transfers_exceeding_daily_limit_declined(auth_headers, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    seed_payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "20000.00"}
    seed_resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=seed_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert seed_resp.status_code in (200, 201), "Seed transfer must succeed"
    src_balance_mid = <get_balance>(src, auth_headers)
    tgt_balance_mid = <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "10000.00"}
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"] == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert <get_balance>(src, auth_headers) == src_balance_mid
    assert <get_balance>(tgt, auth_headers) == tgt_balance_mid
